title: Fraud Triage
summary: 0.978 AUC; caught fraud the base model missed.
hook: A two-stage pipeline that catches what the model misses.
brief: Built on 284,807 real card transactions. XGBoost scores every transaction at scale; a reasoning layer reviews only the high-risk flags and writes a structured, analyst-ready verdict. Weighted loss took AUC from 0.60 to 0.978, and in testing the second layer caught a fraud the model had scored a perfect 0.000000.
method: Weighted-loss XGBoost plus a triage layer with strict JSON output
outcome: Industry-grade AUC with a reasoning trail analysts can audit

title: Corporate Analytics
summary: 30% faster executive reviews via custom dashboards.
hook: 30% faster executive reviews via custom dashboards.
brief: Executive Power BI dashboards built on live B2B data streams for TapInnovations, replacing a legacy spreadsheet workflow. Custom DAX measures gave leadership direct visibility into cross-functional throughput, and weekly performance reviews accelerated by 30%.
method: Custom DAX measures on live B2B data streams
outcome: Weekly executive reviews 30% faster, spreadsheets retired

title: Hospital Readmission
summary: 91% recall on readmissions via threshold tuning.
hook: 91% recall on readmissions through threshold tuning.
brief: Readmission risk on 25,000 anonymized encounters, a Python rebuild of a tool-based analysis. Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting were compared; the champion (HistGradientBoosting, ROC-AUC 0.66) was threshold-tuned to catch 91% of readmissions, the costly case to miss. Prior inpatient and emergency visits were the dominant drivers.
method: Three-model comparison with a recall-tuned decision threshold

title: Auto Insurance Churn
brief: Five classifiers on 92,849 policyholder records with severe class imbalance (11.5% churn). A SMOTE pipeline plus a data-leakage fix (dropping a column only set after churn) put Gradient Boosting on top at ROC-AUC 0.70, threshold-tuned for recall, replacing an earlier run that had crowned a near-random model.
method: Five-model comparison on a SMOTE pipeline, champion chosen by ROC-AUC
outcome: Gradient Boosting champion at ROC-AUC 0.70, tuned for recall

title: Supply Chain Opt.
brief: Managed a simulated global supply chain through four years of high demand volatility. A hybrid sourcing strategy with statistical inventory buffering held a 98% service level with zero stockouts while maximizing gross margin.
method: Hybrid sourcing with statistical inventory buffering
outcome: 34% margin at a 98% service level, zero stockouts

title: Bullwhip Mitigation
brief: A four-year product lifecycle and supplier risk simulation. Engineered a hybrid sourcing strategy balancing low-cost overseas suppliers against high-flexibility nearshore ones, managing product design upgrades and volatile demand to close at a 54% cumulative gross margin.
method: Hybrid sourcing balanced across offshore and nearshore suppliers
outcome: 54% cumulative gross margin under the bullwhip effect

title: Prime Air Drone Logistics
hook: A hub-and-spoke drone network covering 7.9% of Massachusetts.
brief: Last-mile feasibility study for suburban drone delivery in Massachusetts. A multi-criteria scoring model (demand, tech/income readiness, operational feasibility) ranks candidate cities and sites three hubs (Cambridge, Worcester, Springfield), one per region, within the MK30's 7.5-mile radius; a reproducible coverage model projects ~1,900 packages/day across 550,000 residents, rendered as an interactive map.
method: Multi-criteria scoring (demand, readiness, feasibility) with a reproducible coverage model
outcome: Three-hub model reaching ~550K residents (7.9% of MA), with an interactive map

title: Strategy Summit
brief: Ran a simulated sensor manufacturer across R&D, marketing, production, and finance for five simulated years. Calibrated pricing tiers and capacity against rate-sensitive demand, recovering the share price from $1.40 to $8.17 and finishing top-3 in cumulative profit. Recognized for an alternating high-tech product strategy.
method: Pricing and capacity calibration across 5 simulated years
outcome: Share price from $1.40 to $8.17, top-3 cumulative profit

title: Analysis on Heart Disease Predictors
hook: ~82% accuracy, deployed as a Flask app.
brief: End-to-end capstone comparing five classifiers on the 13-feature clinical heart dataset. Random Forest was the strongest at ~82% accuracy; a top-3 majority-vote ensemble landed just behind, served through a Flask web app that scores risk from a 13-field form.
method: Five-model comparison with a top-3 majority-vote ensemble, served via Flask
outcome: ~82% Random Forest accuracy; ensemble just behind, in a live app

title: CPK Production Planner
summary: Order report in, make lists and pick sheets out, across 36 locations.
hook: Paste the day's order report, get every production sheet.
brief: A live tool that replaces a hand-built 39-tab spreadsheet for a commissary kitchen. Paste the daily order recap and it builds the DC and retail make lists, plus bread, prep, fruit, packout and stickers, across 36 campus locations. It runs entirely in the browser, nothing is sent anywhere.
method: Parses the order recap and pivots it into per-station production sheets, all client-side
outcome: A 39-tab manual spreadsheet replaced by an instant, in-browser planner
